result:
All five functions are implemented in `problems.py`, using only the standard library (`re`, `collections.Counter`, `collections.deque`). No function names, signatures, or docstrings were changed.

## What each function does

**`parse_duration`** тАФ A single ordered regex `(?:(\d+)h)?(?:(\d+)m)?(?:(\d+)s)?` matched with `fullmatch` does nearly all the validation work for free:
- The baked-in `hтЖТmтЖТs` order rejects out-of-order (`30m1h`) and duplicate (`1h2h`) inputs.
- `fullmatch` rejects trailing garbage (`1h!`), unknown units (`1x`), whitespace, and bare numbers (`45`).
- `\d+` (no sign) rejects negatives (`-5s`); requiring digits rejects bare units (`h`).
- A non-string or all-`None` match (empty string) raises `ValueError`.

**`luhn_valid`** тАФ Strips spaces/hyphens, returns `False` (never raises) on empty or non-digit input, then doubles every second digit from the right (subtracting 9 on overflow) and checks `total % 10 == 0`. Verified against `"0"`тЖТTrue, `"5"`тЖТFalse, and the known-valid Visa number.

**`flatten`** тАФ Recursive helper accumulating into a fresh result dict; empty nested dicts contribute nothing, lists/scalars/`None`/`bool` are kept as-is, and the input is never mutated (only read).

**`top_k_frequent`** тАФ Counts with `Counter`, then sorts by `(-count, word)` so highest frequency wins and ties break alphabetically (case-sensitive, since `"A" < "a"`). `k <= 0` тЖТ `[]`; `k > distinct` returns all.

**`rolling_max`** тАФ Monotonic deque holding indices with strictly-decreasing values; the front is always the current window's max. `window <= 0` raises `ValueError`, `window > len` returns `[]`.

## Verification
Beyond the docstring examples, I ran a randomized brute-force cross-check of `rolling_max` (500 cases vs. naive `max()` per window, all passed) and a 2M-element benchmark (**0.72s**, confirming genuine O(n) тАФ a per-window `max()` would be ~1.3 trillion ops here). The workspace is clean: only `problems.py` is modified, no commits created.
